content.problemStatement:
Chef found a matrix AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM), where for each row rrr and column ccc, the cell in row rrr and column ccc (denoted by (r,c)(r,c)(r,c)) contains an integer Ar,cA_{r,c}Ar,c‚Äč.

This matrix has two interesting properties:


The integers in each row form a non-decreasing sequence, i.e. for each valid iii, Ai,1‚Č§Ai,2‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§Ai,MA_{i,1} \leq A_{i,2} \leq \ldots \leq A_{i,M}Ai,1‚Äč‚Č§Ai,2‚Äč‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§Ai,M‚Äč.
The integers in each column also form a non-decreasing sequence, i.e. for each valid jjj, A1,j‚Č§A2,j‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§AN,jA_{1,j} \leq A_{2,j} \leq \ldots \leq A_{N,j}A1,j‚Äč‚Č§A2,j‚Äč‚Č§‚Ä¶‚Č§AN,j‚Äč.
A KKK-worthy submatrix is a square submatrix of AAA, i.e. a submatrix with lll rows and lll columns, for any integer lll, such that the average of all the integers in this submatrix is ‚Č•K\geq K‚Č•K.

Chef wants you to find the number of KKK-worthy submatrices of AAA.
constraints: the sum of N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (15 points):** the sum of N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
**Subtask #2 (25 points):** the sum of N‚čÖMN \cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases does not exceed 4‚čÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,Ai,3,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i,1}, A_{i,2}, A_{i,3}, \ldots, A_{i,M}Ai,1‚Äč,Ai,2‚Äč,Ai,3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of KKK-worthy submatrices of AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The following are the seven 444-worthy submatrices:

[3445]
\begin{bmatrix}
3 & 4 \\
4 & 5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[34‚Äč45‚Äč]
with average 444; this matrix occurs only once
[4555]\begin{bmatrix}
4 & 5 \\
5 & 5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[45‚Äč55‚Äč]
with average 4.754.754.75; this matrix also occurs only once
[4]\begin{bmatrix}
4 \\
\end{bmatrix}[4‚Äč]
with average 444; we find this matrix twice in AAA
[5]\begin{bmatrix}
5 \\
\end{bmatrix}[5‚Äč]
with average 555; we find this matrix 333 times in AAA
